prompt: Our userService.js has a nasty intermittent bug. Sometimes when two users hit the API at nearly the same time, user A gets user B's permissions, or vice versa. The logging middleware (which calls getCurrentUser()) sometimes logs the wrong user too. I added null checks for the user object — didn't help, the user IS there, it's just the wrong user. I also wrapped the whole getEnrichedUser in a try/catch thinking it was a failed fetch — no errors are thrown, the data is just wrong. This only happens under concurrent load, never in single-user testing.
assertions: Lists at least 2 distinct hypotheses for what could be causing the bug
Proposes a diagnostic step or experiment before jumping to the final fix
Explains what evidence confirms the root cause before applying the fix
Correctly identifies the shared mutable currentUser module-level variable as the race condition
Fix removes or localizes the module-level state so concurrent calls don't interfere
